content.title: Newcomer's Prompt Collections - Repository
content.description: A launchpad for several prompt created by different LLMs.  It's a good way to find starter prompts, compare prompting styles, and give you a sense of the variety of design decisions the models might make.

content.description: Advanced prompting techniques and examples extracted from Frontier Model system cards (April 2026)

content.description: Thinking-Together Series:  What happens when we shift our thinking from AI as an assistant, to AI as a collaborator.  A lot!

content.title: AI Training for University Administrators
content.description: All AI training is fundamentally the same, it is just the context that changes.

content.title: The World of Chat Bots - Presentation
content.description: Explore an introduction to chat bots, built by the Claude chat dev bot.  Claude like some of the other models can build artifacts and host them like this one.

content.title: The Art of Prompting - Course
content.description: When you are chatting with a chatbot (LLM) you are "prompting"  This skillset is evolving rapidly, but this little course will give you a solid foundation.  This was built by the Manus tool.

content.title: Prompting for Professionals - Course
content.description: A Claude based course you can use to dig deeper into prompting patterns and strategies.

content.title: Prompting Patterns - Repository
content.description: Twenty prompting patterns to help you build your own.

content.title: Prompt Engineering Hub - Repository
content.description:
More advanced prompting examples focused on General Text, Reasoning, Coding,
Data Analysis, Multi-modal with a Dynamic refresh option to search for new patterns.

content.title: How LLMs learn to talk - Presentation
content.description: A non-technical take on explaining how LLMs learn to communicate.

content.title: Newcomer Bot Hub
content.description: Use the Newcomer Bot Hub to explore mainstream and edge bots from leading AI labs around the world.  Use the notes feature to track information about the bots.  You info is saved locally.

content.title: Mainstream Chatbot feature compare
content.description: Feature compare of the Frontier bots

content.title: Our Vibe Coding Tools List
content.description: What is Vibe coding?  It is a big part of the future of computer programing, without coding.  If you've ever had fun building an Excel macro, or writing a formula, you should be vibe coding.   This list is tools we use.  The list is not exhaustive and many details are not current.  The tool landscape, especially in the vibe coding space, changes almost weekly.

content.title: LM Arena Model Battle Playground
content.description: Explore, play, build and vote on frontier AI with models.

content.title: LM Council - a multi-model site
content.description: A unique offering in the LLM space, allowing you to access multiple models (8) simultaneously and have those models communicate amongst themselves.  There is also access to built in benchmarking data, image gen, transcription and more.  The tool is built and managed by a very small independent research team in the UK,  They also host the @AIExplained YouTube channel, one of my goto research channels.  There is a limited use free tier.

content.title: Google Labs
content.description: A place to explore interesting and quirky AI tools and experiments from the teams at Google that brought you Notebook LM, Flow and Firebase.  As you look through the tools, look for the tag "Try it now" and that's a good chance it'll be available.

content.title: AI Velocity - Presentation
content.description: A quick look at AI growth velocity and links to more data

content.title: AI Explosion - presentation video (no sound)
content.description: Built using the latest AI industry acceleration numbers.  Built on the Emergent.AI system

content.title: State of AI in early 2026 - Report
content.description: Stanford's 2026 AI index

content.title: Large Language Models for the Curious Beginner
content.description: The best description of Large Language Models and the Transformer Architecture you will ever find.  (Be sure to watch the video.)

content.title: AI Adaption vs. Adoption Whitepaper - a Google Illuminate Podcast
content.description: A look into how organizations should frame AI if they want to maximize it's strategic potential.  Presented using Google Lab's Illuminate AI Podcast tool.

content.title: MAI  (Microsoft AI) Playground
content.description: The term Playground is typically used by frontier model builders to allow people early access to various features and models often free and always interesting to explore.

content.title: Ai2 Playground
content.description: The Ai2 Playground allows you to interact with Ai2's large language models in a user-friendly interface without the need to download anything. You can use it to test LLM capabilities and generate text based on prompts.

content.title: Adaption vs. Adoption - Paper
content.description: A whitepaper discussing the merits of Adapting with AI vs. strictly Adopting AI tech.

content.title: AI & Leadership - Paper
content.description: Written in early 2023, this whitepaper outlines areas of focus for leaders as the AI era begins unfolding.  Not much was written at that time, as we were just getting started.

content.description: See example working hubs  1 and 2

content.description: Brought to you by the creators of Notebook LM, Google Illuminate is a podcast creation tool designed for researchers and academics but makes a great tool for general use as well.

content.description: Walking Gemini through a deck build  (may not be exposed to the public)

glossaryTerms.definition: Hypothetical AI systems with human-level or greater intelligence across all cognitive tasks.
The simulation of human intelligence processes by computer systems, including learning, reasoning, and self-correction.
Systematic errors in AI outputs that reflect prejudices present in training data or model design.
A prompting technique that encourages AI models to break down complex problems into step-by-step reasoning.
The maximum amount of text (in tokens) an AI model can consider when generating a response.
The study of moral principles and values that should guide the development and use of AI systems.
A learning approach where AI models learn from just a few examples rather than extensive training data.
The process of further training a pre-trained model on a specific dataset to improve its performance for particular tasks.
When AI generates confident but incorrect or nonsensical information that appears plausible.
The process of using a trained AI model to make predictions or generate outputs based on new input data.
A type of AI model trained on vast amounts of text data to understand and generate human-like text.
A subset of AI that enables systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed.
The interaction between computers and human language, enabling machines to understand, interpret, and generate human language.
A computing system inspired by biological neural networks, consisting of interconnected nodes (neurons) that process information.
The art of crafting effective inputs (prompts) to guide AI models toward desired outputs.
A parameter that controls the randomness of AI-generated text, with lower values producing more deterministic outputs.
The basic unit of text that AI models process, which can be a word, part of a word, or character.
A deep learning architecture that uses attention mechanisms to process sequential data, revolutionizing NLP.
The dataset used to teach AI models patterns, relationships, and knowledge required for making predictions or decisions.
The ability of AI models to perform tasks without seeing examples, using only task descriptions.

aiTools.name: ChatGPT
aiTools.description: A conversational AI assistant by OpenAI that can answer questions, write content, and assist with various tasks.

aiTools.name: Claude
aiTools.description: An AI assistant by Anthropic designed to be helpful, harmless, and honest with strong safety features.

aiTools.name: Microsoft Copilot
aiTools.description: A great daily assistant.  Many integrations across the Windows platform and one of the highest levels of free access.  Don't forget to use the Microsoft Edge Copilot assistant.

aiTools.name: Perplexity AI
aiTools.description: An AI-powered search engine that provides direct answers with cited sources for factual queries.

aiTools.name: Google AI Studio
aiTools.description: Explore and build on Google's diverse set of models and tools.  Vibe , graphics, audio, etc... you'll find it all here.

aiTools.name: The Other Chatbots - Big guys and little guys
aiTools.description:
9 more and some you've probably never seen:
Le Chat (Mistral), Qwen Studio, Together Chat, Kimi AI, Z.ai, Inception Chat, Chat Jimmy, DeepSeek, Meta

aiTools.name: Notebook LM
aiTools.description: Googles Notebook LM is a bedrock research, students and business people all around the world.

aiTools.name: Elicit - AI for Scientific Research
aiTools.description: Search and chat with scientific research papers

aiTools.name: Emergent Mind - Frontier AI Research Explorer
aiTools.description: Emergent Mind a curated, continuously updated interface for discovering the newest, most influential, and most-discussed AI research papers.  Auto infographic build feature very powerful.

aiTools.name: Learning about Neural Networks
aiTools.description:
Not so much an AI tool but a treasure trove of learning resources about Neural Networks, LLMs and Mathematics.
Brought to you by the genius behind 3Blue1Brown

aiTools.name: Poe bot hub
aiTools.description:
Poe is not a single model — it's a hub for many models.
Some bots are official (OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta).
Many bots are user-created, with unknown safety, accuracy, or moderation.
